tasks_completed.description: Add pre-dispatch baseline capture to commands/dev.md Step 8
tasks_completed.changes: Added 'Pre-dispatch baseline capture' block with git rev-parse HEAD and git status --porcelain commands; added baseline_head_sha and baseline_dirty_snapshot fields to the dispatch payload body
tasks_completed.rationale: Without baseline capture, dev has no reference point to compute which files it actually changed vs which were pre-existing. Addresses root cause: no baseline SHA captured at dispatch time.

tasks_completed.description: Mandate git-diff derivation for files_modified in agents/dev.md Output Format
tasks_completed.changes: Added 'Git-diff derivation (MANDATORY)' paragraph; added 'observed_preexisting[]' field description; added 'baseline_head_sha' as top-level dev-report field; added baseline_head_sha and observed_preexisting fields to the JSON schema example
tasks_completed.rationale: Dev agents were performing work-tree inspection instead of diffing, causing inflated file counts. This mandates git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha> as the source of truth.

tasks_completed.description: Add provenance check to agents/qa.md Step 5
tasks_completed.changes: Added 'Provenance check' subsection at the top of Step 5 Regression Testing. Specifies: read baseline_head_sha, compute git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha>, raise critical FAIL with primary_cause: 'dev_implementation' and label 'dev_provenance_violation' for paths absent from diff and baseline_dirty_snapshot. Graceful skip when baseline_head_sha absent.
tasks_completed.rationale: QA is the last line of defense before changelog-analyst and commit chain consume the file list.

tasks_completed.description: Add provenance filter to agents/changelog-analyst.md Phase 2
tasks_completed.changes: Added 'Provenance filter' subsection after dev-report extraction in Phase 2. Specifies: compute git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha>..HEAD (committed diff at changelog-analyst time), classify absent paths as provenance_anomaly, exclude from type/scope/summary enrichment. Graceful skip when baseline_head_sha absent.
tasks_completed.rationale: Commit message attribution must reflect actual changes, not pre-existing files dev found in correct state. The ..HEAD form is critical because changelog-analyst runs post-commit.

tasks_completed.description: Implement skeleton test bodies for all 5 generated acceptance criteria tests
tasks_completed.rationale: All 5 tests replaced TEST_INCOMPLETE sentinels with real grep/read assertions against the modified markdown files. All 5 pass.

git_rationale.root_cause_commit: N/A — spec omission, not a regression from a specific commit
git_rationale.why_issue_occurred: agents/dev.md Output Format was designed for the common case where dev actually edits files; the pre-existing-state scenario (dev confirms correct state without diff) was not anticipated. No baseline SHA was captured at dispatch time and no diff-derivation was mandated.
git_rationale.how_fix_addresses_root: Four additive contract clauses: (1) orchestrator captures baseline SHA at dispatch, (2) dev derives files_modified from git diff not work-tree inspection, (3) QA fails on inflated claims, (4) changelog-analyst excludes anomaly paths from enrichment.

diff_stats.justification_for_overage: Four separate additive insertions across four distinct files, each adding a distinct required contract clause to satisfy a separate acceptance criterion. No individual insertion can be removed without dropping an AC. The 51-line total is ~13 lines per file on average, which is the minimum prose needed to specify the behavior precisely (including the critical diff-command-form distinction between pre-commit and post-commit contexts).

dev.qa_notes: Run pytest tests/generated/20260522-080646-D/ to verify all 5 ACs pass. Grep checks: (1) baseline_head_sha and baseline_dirty_snapshot inside Step 8 dispatch payload in commands/dev.md; (2) observed_preexisting, baseline_head_sha, git diff --name-only in agents/dev.md Output Format; (3) dev_provenance_violation, baseline_head_sha, primary_cause.*dev_implementation in agents/qa.md; (4) provenance_anomaly, baseline_head_sha in agents/changelog-analyst.md Phase 2; (5) graceful skip language (absent or empty, Do NOT raise a FAIL) in both qa.md and changelog-analyst.md.

self_verification.notes: All 5 pytest tests pass: 5 passed in 0.03s. All grep verifications confirmed. No build system for markdown-only changes.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Two findings: (1) [in_scope_real_bug] changelog-analyst Phase 2 provenance filter used git diff --name-only <sha>..HEAD which is wrong at pre-commit time — Phase 2 runs before staging/commit so ..HEAD returns empty set, falsely flagging all legitimate changes as provenance_anomaly. (2) [in_scope_minor] baseline_dirty_snapshot was consumed by QA/changelog but not declared as a mandatory top-level dev-report field in agents/dev.md schema.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Fix 1: Changed changelog-analyst.md provenance filter step 1 from '..HEAD' form to working-tree diff form 'git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha>' with explanatory note why ..HEAD is wrong pre-commit. Fix 2: Added baseline_dirty_snapshot as mandatory top-level dev-report field in agents/dev.md with schema entry. Updated dev-report JSON to include baseline_dirty_snapshot. Updated AC4 test to verify correct diff form.

recommendations: A future cycle (OOS item D C1) could add a hook that enforces the baseline capture at dispatch time rather than relying on the markdown spec alone.

The two-repo scenario (main repo + nested repo at /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude) should be addressed in a follow-up: baseline_head_sha_root and baseline_head_sha_nested may be needed for cycles that touch both repos.
